bleachCharacters.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Characters
bleachCharacters.intro: Bleach follows a large cast of warriors whose loyalties, abilities, and personal histories influence the balance of the spiritual world. These character cards introduce the main heroes, captains, allies, and antagonists.

items.name: Ichigo Kurosaki
items.description: A human who gains Soul Reaper powers from Rukia Kuchiki. He repeatedly enters spiritual conflicts to protect his family, friends, and both the Human World and Soul Society.

items.name: Rukia Kuchiki
items.description: The Soul Reaper who transfers her powers to Ichigo at the beginning of the story. She later becomes a powerful officer and eventually the captain of Squad 13.

items.name: Renji Abarai
items.description: The lieutenant of Squad 6 and Rukia's childhood friend. Renji begins as Ichigo's opponent but becomes one of his most dependable allies.

items.name: Byakuya Kuchiki
items.description: The captain of Squad 6 and head of the noble Kuchiki Clan. His strict devotion to Soul Society law gradually changes through his encounters with Ichigo and Rukia.

items.name: Toshiro Hitsugaya
items.description: A young prodigy who serves as captain of Squad 10. He commands Hyorinmaru, one of the most powerful ice-type Zanpakuto.

items.name: Kenpachi Zaraki
items.description: The battle-loving captain of Squad 11 and holder of the Kenpachi title. He relies on immense spiritual pressure, physical strength, and an instinctive desire to fight powerful opponents.

items.name: Sosuke Aizen
items.description: The former captain of Squad 5 who secretly manipulates events throughout Soul Society. He seeks to transcend the limits separating Soul Reapers, Hollows, and the Soul King.

items.name: Ulquiorra Cifer
items.description: The Fourth Espada in Aizen's army and one of Ichigo's most dangerous opponents. His calm, emotionless outlook is challenged through his interactions with Orihime.

items.name: Gin Ichimaru
items.description: The former captain of Squad 3 who follows Aizen while hiding his true intentions. His constant smile and deceptive behavior make his loyalties difficult to understand.

items.name: Yoruichi Shihoin
items.description: The former captain of Squad 2 and former commander of the Onmitsukido. She is a master of hand-to-hand combat, stealth, and high-speed movement.

items.name: Uryu Ishida
items.description: A skilled Quincy archer and one of Ichigo's closest allies. His Quincy heritage places him at the center of the final conflict with the Wandenreich.

items.name: Orihime Inoue
items.description: Ichigo's classmate and a wielder of the Shun Shun Rikka. Her abilities can reject harmful events, allowing her to heal injuries, create barriers, and protect her companions.

bleachZanpakuto.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Zanpakuto
bleachZanpakuto.intro: A Zanpakuto reflects the soul of its wielder and possesses its own name and spirit. By communicating with that spirit, a Soul Reaper can unlock Shikai and eventually the far more powerful Bankai.

items.name: Zangetsu
items.shikai: A permanently released oversized blade that channels Ichigo's spiritual power into attacks such as Getsuga Tensho.
items.bankai: Its Bankai is Tensa Zangetsu, which compresses Ichigo's power into a smaller blade and greatly increases his speed and combat performance.

items.name: Sode no Shirayuki
items.shikai: Uses a series of dances to freeze targets, create paths of ice, and lower temperatures around the blade.
items.bankai: Hakka no Togame drastically lowers Rukia's body temperature and freezes everything within its effective area.

items.name: Zabimaru
items.shikai: Transforms into a segmented blade that can extend like a whip and strike enemies from a distance.
items.bankai: So-oh Zabimaru creates massive skeletal and baboon-like components that deliver powerful physical and spiritual attacks.

items.name: Senbonzakura
items.shikai: Separates its blade into countless tiny blades that resemble drifting cherry blossom petals.
items.bankai: Senbonzakura Kageyoshi summons enormous blades that divide into millions of controllable fragments and can form several specialized attack modes.

items.name: Kyoka Suigetsu
items.shikai: Places anyone who witnesses its release under Complete Hypnosis, allowing Aizen to manipulate all five senses.

items.name: Hyorinmaru
items.shikai: Creates and controls large quantities of ice and water, often taking the form of an ice dragon.
items.bankai: Daiguren Hyorinmaru forms ice wings, claws, and a tail. Its mature state can freeze both physical matter and the functions of affected objects.

items.name: Katen Kyokotsu
items.shikai: Turns children's games into real combat rules that affect everyone participating within its range.
items.bankai: Katen Kyokotsu: Karamatsu Shinju traps nearby opponents in a four-act tragedy involving shared wounds, illness, drowning, and a final severing attack.

items.name: Ryujin Jakka
items.shikai: Produces overwhelming flames capable of burning large areas and reducing targets to ash.
items.bankai: Zanka no Tachi concentrates its flames into the blade and grants four directional abilities involving extreme heat, destructive contact, defense, and the corpses of previous victims.

items.name: Minazuki
items.shikai: Transforms into a large flying creature that transports injured allies and heals them inside its body.
items.bankai: Minazuki releases a blood-like liquid around Unohana and her sword during close-range combat. Its exact mechanics are not fully explained.

items.name: Nozarashi
items.shikai: Transforms into a huge axe-like blade with enough cutting power to destroy enormous objects and heavily armored opponents.
items.bankai: Turns Kenpachi into a red-skinned berserker with drastically increased strength and cutting power, although he initially struggles to control it.

items.name: Sogyo no Kotowari
items.shikai: Splits into two blades connected by a cord and talismans, allowing Ukitake to absorb and redirect energy attacks.

bleachArcs.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Arcs
bleachArcs.intro: The Bleach storyline expands from supernatural incidents in Karakura Town into wars involving Soul Society, Hueco Mundo, and the Quincy empire. This timeline presents the major canonical arcs in narrative order.

steps.title: Substitute Soul Reaper Arc
steps.description: Ichigo Kurosaki receives Soul Reaper powers from Rukia Kuchiki and begins protecting Karakura Town from Hollows. He meets spiritually aware allies including Orihime Inoue, Yasutora Sado, and Quincy archer Uryu Ishida.

steps.title: Soul Society Arc
steps.description: Rukia is arrested and sentenced to execution for transferring her powers to a human. Ichigo and his friends invade Soul Society, battle members of the Gotei 13, and uncover Sosuke Aizen's hidden conspiracy.

steps.title: Arrancar: The Arrival Arc
steps.description: Aizen creates an army of Arrancar using the Hogyoku and sends them to attack the Human World. Ichigo struggles to control his inner Hollow while training with the Visored.

steps.title: Hueco Mundo Arc
steps.description: Orihime is taken to Aizen's fortress in Hueco Mundo, prompting Ichigo, Uryu, and Chad to launch a rescue mission. They fight numerous Arrancar and Espada while advancing through Las Noches.

steps.title: Turn Back the Pendulum Arc
steps.description: A flashback reveals the events that occurred more than a century earlier in Soul Society. It explains how Aizen's Hollowfication experiments transformed several captains and lieutenants into the Visored.

steps.title: Fake Karakura Town Arc
steps.description: The Gotei 13 confronts Aizen, Gin, Tosen, and the remaining Espada in a replica of Karakura Town. Ichigo ultimately undergoes intense training to gain the power needed for his decisive battle with Aizen.

steps.title: Lost Agent Arc
steps.description: After losing his Soul Reaper abilities, Ichigo meets Kugo Ginjo and the Fullbringer organization Xcution. Their promise to restore his powers develops into a conflict involving stolen Fullbring abilities and Ginjo's past.

steps.title: Thousand-Year Blood War Arc
steps.description: Yhwach and the Wandenreich invade Soul Society, steal Bankai, and declare war on the Soul Reapers. Ichigo discovers the truth about his heritage as the conflict expands to the Soul King Palace and threatens the structure of all worlds.

bleachFactions.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Factions
bleachFactions.intro: Bleach's conflicts are driven by organizations with competing ideas about justice, survival, revenge, and the balance of souls. Some defend the established spiritual order, while others seek to destroy or replace it.

items.name: Gotei 13
items.description: The thirteen military divisions responsible for protecting Soul Society and maintaining the balance between worlds. Each squad is commanded by a captain and usually specializes in a particular style of duty or combat.

items.name: Espada
items.description: The ten highest-ranking Arrancar in Aizen's army, numbered according to their spiritual power. They rule over lesser Arrancar and fight to support Aizen's plan to overthrow the existing order.

items.name: Visored
items.description: Former Soul Reaper captains and lieutenants who gained Hollow powers through Aizen's experiments. They initially live in exile but later help Ichigo control his inner Hollow and oppose Aizen.

items.name: Quincy
items.description: Spiritually aware humans who destroy Hollows using weapons formed from gathered spiritual particles. Their method of completely erasing Hollows threatens the balance of souls and historically brings them into conflict with the Soul Reapers.

items.name: Wandenreich
items.description: A hidden Quincy empire that survives in the shadows of Soul Society and prepares for a thousand-year revenge campaign. Its Sternritter invade Soul Society, steal Bankai, and attempt to reshape the worlds under Yhwach's rule.

items.name: Xcution
items.description: An organization of Fullbringers who claim they want to remove their powers and become ordinary humans. Ginjo instead uses the group to manipulate Ichigo and steal the Fullbring-enhanced Soul Reaper power he develops.

items.name: Fullbringers
items.description: Humans capable of manipulating the souls contained within physical objects. Their powers usually develop after their mothers survive Hollow attacks, but Fullbringers do not belong to one unified organization or share a single goal.

bleachAbilities.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Abilities
bleachAbilities.intro: Bleach features several interconnected power systems based on spiritual energy, race, training, and personal weapons. These abilities range from formal Soul Reaper spells to instinctive Hollow attacks and specialized Quincy defenses.

items.name: Kido
items.description: Kido is the Soul Reapers' system of spiritual spells, activated through incantations and numbered techniques. Hado spells focus on destruction, while Bakudo spells restrain, defend, track, or seal targets; masters such as Tessai, Hachigen, and Aizen can use advanced Kido with reduced or omitted incantations.

items.name: Cero
items.description: Cero is a concentrated blast of spiritual energy commonly fired by Menos Hollows and Arrancar. Its power, speed, color, and firing point vary by user, with stronger variants including Gran Rey Cero and Cero Oscuras.

items.name: Hierro
items.description: Hierro hardens an Arrancar's skin by concentrating spiritual energy throughout the body. Its durability differs between individuals, with fighters such as Nnoitra Gilga possessing exceptionally strong Hierro.

items.name: Hollowfication
items.description: Hollowfication grants Hollow characteristics and power to a Soul Reaper, often producing a mask and a major increase in speed, strength, and spiritual pressure. Ichigo and the Visored use controlled forms of this power, although losing control can trigger a more complete Hollow transformation.

items.name: Bankai
items.description: Bankai is the second and final release of a Soul Reaper's Zanpakuto, normally requiring years of training to achieve and master. Each Bankai reflects its user's spirit and can dramatically transform the weapon, surroundings, or wielder's combat abilities.

items.name: Getsuga Tensho
items.description: Getsuga Tensho is Ichigo Kurosaki's signature technique, releasing condensed spiritual energy from Zangetsu as a powerful crescent-shaped attack. Ichigo can use different forms of it through his Shikai, Bankai, Hollow-enhanced state, and later dual-blade powers.

items.name: Shunpo
items.description: Shunpo is a Soul Reaper high-speed movement technique that allows the user to cross distances faster than ordinary vision can follow. Yoruichi Shihoin, Soi Fon, and Byakuya Kuchiki are among its most accomplished practitioners.

items.name: Sonido
items.description: Sonido is the Arrancar equivalent of high-speed movement and often produces a distinctive sound when used. Skilled Arrancar can use it to evade attacks, create afterimages, and reposition before an opponent can react.

items.name: Hirenkyaku and Blut Vene
items.description: Hirenkyaku lets Quincy travel rapidly by riding currents of spiritual particles beneath their feet. Blut Vene directs spiritual energy through the user's blood vessels to greatly increase defense, while its offensive counterpart, Blut Arterie, strengthens attacks.

items.name: Fullbring
items.description: Fullbring allows spiritually aware humans to manipulate the soul contained within physical matter. Each Fullbringer develops a personal ability connected to an emotionally significant object, such as Ginjo's Substitute Soul Reaper badge or Tsukishima's bookmark.

bleachBattles.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Battles
bleachBattles.intro: Bleach battles combine swordsmanship, evolving transformations, tactical abilities, and personal conflicts. These major fights introduce defining techniques, resolve long-running rivalries, and determine the outcome of entire story arcs.

items.name: Ichigo vs Byakuya
items.description: Ichigo confronts Byakuya at Sokyoku Hill and reveals his newly achieved Bankai, Tensa Zangetsu. After Ichigo's inner Hollow briefly takes control, the two fighters exchange final attacks and Byakuya concedes defeat.

items.name: Ichigo vs Ulquiorra
items.description: Ulquiorra overwhelms Ichigo with his second Resurreccion and leaves him near death in Las Noches. Ichigo then transforms into a powerful uncontrolled Hollow form, defeats Ulquiorra, and regains awareness shortly before Ulquiorra disintegrates.

items.name: Ichigo vs Aizen
items.description: After training in the Dangai, Ichigo returns with power far beyond Aizen's evolved forms. He uses the Final Getsuga Tensho and Mugetsu to weaken Aizen, allowing Kisuke Urahara's hidden Kido seal to imprison him.

items.name: Ichigo vs Ginjo
items.description: Ichigo regains his Soul Reaper powers with help from the Gotei 13 and battles Kugo Ginjo in their Bankai forms. Ichigo defeats Ginjo, but later recognizes the loneliness and resentment that drove the former Substitute Soul Reaper.

items.name: Kenpachi vs Nnoitra
items.description: Nnoitra's durable Hierro and six-armed Resurreccion initially withstand Kenpachi's attacks. Kenpachi eventually uses two-handed kendo, delivers a devastating strike, and defeats the Fifth Espada.

items.name: Yamamoto vs Wonderweiss
items.description: Wonderweiss is revealed to have been modified specifically to seal the flames of Ryujin Jakka. Yamamoto destroys him with his bare hands, survives the resulting explosion, and uses Hado 96 against Aizen, although Aizen escapes the attack.

items.name: Shunsui vs Starrk
items.description: Shunsui battles Coyote Starrk using Katen Kyokotsu's dangerous children's games while several other fighters enter the conflict. Shunsui ultimately exploits the rules of his Shikai and lands the decisive strike against the First Espada.

items.name: Byakuya vs Tsukishima
items.description: Tsukishima uses Book of the End to insert himself into Byakuya's past and learn every established technique. Byakuya rejects that emotional manipulation and kills him with a close-range attack formed from scattered Senbonzakura petals.

items.name: Rukia vs Aaroniero
items.description: Aaroniero uses Kaien Shiba's appearance and memories to destabilize Rukia during their battle in Las Noches. Rukia overcomes her guilt and defeats him by extending Sode no Shirayuki through his head with her third dance.

items.name: Ichigo vs Yhwach
items.description: Yhwach repeatedly uses The Almighty to alter possible futures and neutralize Ichigo's attacks. With assistance from Aizen's illusions and Uryu's Still Silver arrow, Ichigo lands the final strike that defeats the Quincy emperor.

bleachEpisodes.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Episodes
bleachEpisodes.intro: The original Bleach television series contains manga-based episodes, anime-original filler stories, and several ranges that mix both types of material. This guide highlights the largest story sections rather than every individual filler episode.

items.description: Ichigo receives Soul Reaper powers from Rukia, protects Karakura Town, and begins learning about Hollows and the Soul Society.
Ichigo's group enters the Soul Society to rescue Rukia, leading to battles with the Gotei 13 and the revelation of Aizen's betrayal.
The anime-original Bount storyline follows immortal humans who consume souls and later invade the Soul Society under Jin Kariya.

items.arc: Arrancar Arrival and Early Hueco Mundo
items.description: Aizen's Arrancar appear in the Human World, Ichigo trains with the Visored, and Orihime is taken to Hueco Mundo. The range includes some anime-original episodes.
Muramasa causes numerous Zanpakuto spirits to manifest and rebel against their Soul Reaper masters. The later episodes continue with stories about the remaining manifested spirits.
The Hueco Mundo and Fake Karakura Town conflicts reach their climax, ending with Ichigo's final confrontation against Aizen. Several anime-original episodes interrupt the main storyline.
Artificial copies of Soul Reapers infiltrate the Soul Society while Ichigo's remaining spiritual power continues to fade.
Ichigo meets the Fullbringers of Xcution while searching for a way to regain his powers, culminating in his battle with Kugo Ginjo.

bleachManga.title: BLEACH Mirrors High Manga
bleachManga.intro: Tite Kubo's Bleach manga was collected into 74 volumes containing 686 numbered chapters. The major story arcs sometimes begin or end partway through a collected volume, so some volume ranges overlap at arc boundaries.

items.description: Ichigo meets Rukia Kuchiki, becomes a Substitute Soul Reaper, and begins protecting his family and town from Hollows.
The opening storyline introduces Ichigo's allies, the Quincy Uryu Ishida, and the events leading to Rukia's arrest.
Ichigo and his friends invade the Soul Society to prevent Rukia's execution. The arc concludes with Aizen revealing his conspiracy and escaping to Hueco Mundo.

Aizen creates an Arrancar army, Orihime is taken to Hueco Mundo, and the conflict expands into the battle over Karakura Town. The storyline ends after Ichigo sacrifices his Soul Reaper powers to defeat Aizen.
A powerless Ichigo encounters Xcution and develops Fullbring while attempting to return to battle. The arc reveals Kugo Ginjo's history as the first Substitute Soul Reaper.
Yhwach and the Wandenreich invade the Soul Society, forcing the Soul Reapers to confront the Quincy and the hidden history of their world.
The final volume resolves the battle against Yhwach and concludes with an epilogue showing the characters and their children years later.
The full Bleach manga spans 74 collected volumes and 686 numbered chapters, covering Ichigo Kurosaki's complete original journey.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about BLEACH Mirrors High and the Bleach universe.

questions.question: What is BLEACH Mirrors High?
questions.answer: BLEACH Mirrors High is a fan-made wiki and resource hub dedicated to the Bleach anime and manga by Tite Kubo, covering characters, Zanpakuto, story arcs, factions, abilities, battles, episodes, and manga chapters.

questions.question: Is BLEACH Mirrors High an official Bleach website?
questions.answer: No. BLEACH Mirrors High is an unofficial fan resource and is not affiliated with Tite Kubo, Shueisha, Studio Pierrot, or VIZ Media. All Bleach rights remain with their respective owners.

questions.question: Who is the main character of Bleach?
questions.answer: The main character of Bleach is Ichigo Kurosaki, a teenager who becomes a Substitute Soul Reaper after receiving powers from Rukia Kuchiki. BLEACH Mirrors High covers his full journey from beginning to end.

questions.question: How many Bleach manga volumes and chapters are there?
questions.answer: The Bleach manga by Tite Kubo spans 74 collected volumes and 686 numbered chapters, concluding with the Thousand-Year Blood War arc that BLEACH Mirrors High breaks down in detail.

questions.question: What is a Zanpakuto in Bleach?
questions.answer: A Zanpakuto is the signature sword of a Soul Reaper in Bleach, with its own name and spirit that can be released into Shikai and Bankai forms. BLEACH Mirrors High catalogs the most iconic Zanpakuto and their wielders.

questions.question: Where can I watch or read Bleach officially?
questions.answer: You can follow official Bleach releases through the official anime site and VIZ Media. BLEACH Mirrors High links to these official sources so you can support the original creators.
